CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-08-2025 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-18-2025 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Nov-04-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar May-13-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Long Cao, Esq. 310-933-4271 Quill & Arrow LLP for Plaintiff.
Cameron Major, Esq. 949-777-6032 The Eskrine Law Group for Defendant.

ARGUED. Mr. Cao does not contest the court's tentative ruling and requests to continue the trial. Mr. Major has nothing to add. The Court informs Counsel to bring the request to Dept. 206. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT GENERAL MOTORS LLC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

Defendant General Motors LLC's motion for summary adjudication is GRANTED.

(see word minutes for full details)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-24-2025 Continued To Jun-23-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant To Comply With Responses To Inspection Demands Pursuant To Ccp 2031.320(A)
Off Calendar Feb-04-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MANUEL JIMENEZ's Motion To Compel Defendant To Comply With Responses To Inspection Demands Pursuant To Ccp 2031.320(A).

Plaintiff Manuel Dominic Jimenez's Motion To Compel Defendant To Comply With Responses To Inspection Demands Pursuant To CCP 2031.320(A) is GRANTED.

(see word minutes for full details)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and drop a hard paper copy in Dept. 302's inbox for the court's review and signature.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-04-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MANUEL JIMENEZ's Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Complaint.

Plaintiff's motion for leave to file a First Amended Complaint ("FAC") is GRANTED. Plaintiff shall file and serve the FAC by February 11, 2025.

The motion substantially complies with CRC 3.1324. "Great liberality is indulged in matters of amendment to the end that lawsuits may be determined upon their merits." (Desny v. Wilder (1956) 46 Cal.2d 715, 751.) The proposed amendments do not change the tenor of the case and defendant has not demonstrated cognizable prejudice. The trial was recently continued to June 23, 2025. Defendant argues that the amended or new claims fail as a matter of law, but the parties can test the viability of the amended or new claims after amendment on noticed motions in which the issues are squared. (California Casualty Gen. Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 281 [preferable practice is to grant leave to amend and allow the parties to subsequently test the viability of the claim] (disapproved on other grounds in Kransco v. American Empire Surplus Lines Ins. Co. (2000) 23 Cal.4th 390); see also Rodriguez, 17 Cal.5th at 206 [noting possible viability of California Uniform Commercial Code claim].)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and drop a hard paper copy in Dept. 302's inbox for the court's review and signature.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-28-2023 Set For Trial On Feb-24-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
